conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
I’m okay, I suppose. Just a bit tired. I haven’t been sleeping well, but I think it’s just stress from work.
You mentioned being tired and not sleeping well—what’s been keeping you up?
Just work mostly. I have a lot on my plate, so my brain won't switch off.
When your mind is racing about work, does it make it hard to get excited about other things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, a bit. I used to love gardening, but now it just feels like another task to get through.
When you think about the future, does it feel like things will get better, or does it seem kind of stuck?
It feels stuck. I’m just waiting for things to settle down so I can get back to normal.
With your mind racing about work, do you find it harder to focus on things you need to get done?
Definitely. I'll be doing something simple and suddenly I'll realize I forgot what I was doing.
When you're feeling stuck and your mind is racing, do you find yourself getting more irritable or on edge with people?
Yeah, I get short with people sometimes. I know it’s not their fault, but I just can’t help it.
When you get short with people, do you notice it happening more at work or at home?
Both, really. I get frustrated with my colleagues, and sometimes I just snap at my partner.
When you snap at your partner, what happens after—do you feel bad about it, or is it just part of the frustration?
I feel bad afterwards. I know I shouldn’t take it out on them, but I just can’t help it when I’m in that state.
When you feel bad after snapping, what do you usually do to try and make things right?
I apologize and explain it wasn't their fault. I know I need to work on managing my temper better.
When you're in that state where you can't help snapping, what does it feel like inside—is it more like a wave of frustration, or something else?
It’s like a storm in my head. I feel like I'm drowning in thoughts and can't switch off.
When you feel like you're drowning in thoughts, does it ever make you feel like you just want to shut everything out?
Yeah, sometimes. I just want to escape from it all and recharge before I can deal with anything properly.
